titleStmt.title: Letter from James Brown to Malachy J. Kelly, 15 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Brown

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from solicitor James Brown to Malachy J. Kelly (1851-1916), the Chief Crown Solicitor. Brown explains how it is not clear whether the brother of the deceased (Elizabeth Hogg) is still alive and so will wait until he hears word before proceeding with an application on behalf of his client, John Duff.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/3888

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Offices:-
Broad Street
Magherafelt 15th April 1916 Dear Sir, E Hogg deceased I am in receipt of yours of 14th inst and beg to say that
Mr Millar Solicitor, Cookstown has written me that he has sent a Power
Of Attorney to a brother of deceased who he thinks is alive but he is not sure. My Client Mr john duff is convinced the man is dead and so
is every one else. I enclose your Mr Millar's letter which please return. In meantime I can go no further with my application till
Mr Millar gets a reply. Yours faithfully James Brown The Chief Crown Solicitor
Chief Secretary's Office
The Castle
Dublin.
